walkthrough.title: Home Alone Anomaly Walkthrough
walkthrough.subtitle: Follow the full sequence of inspections, visitor encounters, and survival decisions. Each step explains what to check before continuing.

steps.title: Learn the Normal House Layout
steps.description: Before the dangerous events begin, inspect the rooms, doors, windows, furniture, and decorations. Remembering the normal layout makes later visual changes easy to recognize.
steps.survivalTip: Do not rush past the opening section, because small environmental details become important later.

steps.title: Begin the Night Routine
steps.description: Move through the house and complete the available interactions while listening for door knocks, movement, and other audio cues. Return to the main areas regularly instead of remaining in one room.
steps.survivalTip: Keep a repeatable inspection route so you can notice what changed between checks.

steps.title: Inspect Every Visitor Carefully
steps.description: When someone appears at the door, observe their appearance and read all dialogue before deciding. Look for unnatural behavior, inconsistent statements, or details that do not match the situation.
steps.survivalTip: A familiar-looking visitor should still be treated as suspicious when their behavior feels wrong.

steps.title: Check the House After Each Encounter
steps.description: After dealing with a visitor, inspect the interior again. Anomalies may appear as changed objects, unusual figures, altered lighting, or unexpected movement.
steps.survivalTip: Compare the current room with what you saw during the opening inspection.

steps.title: Avoid Dangerous Interactions
steps.description: Do not approach suspicious figures or interact with clearly abnormal events unless the game requires it. Create distance and use the safest available response when an encounter becomes threatening.
steps.survivalTip: Curiosity can trigger an early failure, so survival should take priority over exploration.

steps.title: Continue the Observation Cycle
steps.description: Repeat the door check, dialogue review, and house inspection process as the night progresses. Later encounters demand closer attention because the warning signs can become less obvious.
steps.survivalTip: Recheck windows, hallways, and rooms that appeared normal during the previous cycle.

steps.title: Handle the Final Encounters
steps.description: Near the end of the night, use everything learned from earlier visitors and anomalies. Reject clearly unsafe choices and avoid unnecessary risks before the final sequence begins.
steps.survivalTip: One careless decision late in the run can end the game before the final scene.

steps.title: Reach the Final Scene
steps.description: Survive the remaining sequence without inviting danger inside or approaching active threats. The final scene begins after the required encounters and inspection stages have been completed.
steps.survivalTip: Stay focused until control is fully removed or the ending sequence clearly starts.

visitorsGuide.title: Home Alone Anomaly Visitors Guide
visitorsGuide.subtitle: Use appearance, dialogue, and behavior to decide whether a visitor is safe. Never rely on a familiar face alone.

cards.title: Normal-Looking Visitor
cards.description: A visitor may look ordinary and speak calmly without showing an immediate visual anomaly.
cards.warningSigns: Their explanation may still be vague, inconsistent, or unrelated to the reason they came to the house.
cards.recommendedAction: Read every dialogue line and only continue when both their appearance and story remain consistent.

cards.title: Familiar-Looking Visitor
cards.description: Some visitors may appear recognizable or attempt to gain trust through familiarity.
cards.warningSigns: Unnatural expressions, strange wording, unusual timing, or behavior that does not match the person they resemble.
cards.recommendedAction: Judge the visitor by current evidence rather than recognition and refuse entry when details do not match.

cards.title: Visitor With Abnormal Features
cards.description: A dangerous visitor can display distorted facial details, unnatural proportions, or other visible abnormalities.
cards.warningSigns: Eyes, mouth, posture, movement, or body shape appear noticeably wrong.
cards.recommendedAction: Do not allow the visitor inside and avoid prolonged interaction after identifying the anomaly.

cards.title: Aggressive Visitor
cards.description: An unsafe visitor may pressure the player, become threatening, or demand an immediate response.
cards.warningSigns: Hostile language, repeated demands, sudden changes in tone, or attempts to prevent careful inspection.
cards.recommendedAction: Do not respond to pressure by opening the door; choose the option that keeps the house secured.

cards.title: Visitor With an Inconsistent Story
cards.description: The visitor gives a reason for arriving, but parts of the explanation conflict with their behavior or the situation.
cards.warningSigns: Contradictory dialogue, missing details, or statements that change during the conversation.
cards.recommendedAction: Treat contradictions as a danger signal and deny access.

cards.title: Silent or Unresponsive Visitor
cards.description: A person may remain unusually quiet or fail to respond normally during the door interaction.
cards.warningSigns: Long silence, fixed staring, limited dialogue, or no believable explanation for being present.
cards.recommendedAction: Keep the door closed and end the encounter without approaching further.

cards.title: Visitor Appearing at an Unusual Time
cards.description: A visitor can arrive when their presence does not fit the current point in the night.
cards.warningSigns: The timing feels unexplained, the visitor acts as though the situation is normal, or their reason for arriving is weak.
cards.recommendedAction: Use the arrival time as an additional clue and reject the visitor when it conflicts with their story.

cards.title: Visitor Followed by a House Change
cards.description: The danger may become clear only after the conversation ends and the player returns inside.
cards.warningSigns: New sounds, altered rooms, unusual figures, changed lighting, or objects that were not present before.
cards.recommendedAction: Inspect the house immediately after every encounter and respond to any new anomaly before continuing.

anomaliesGuide.title: Home Alone Anomaly Anomalies Guide
anomaliesGuide.subtitle: Compare each room with its normal state and react when people, objects, lighting, or sounds change unexpectedly.

cards.title: Unexpected Figure
cards.location: Hallways, rooms, windows, or other visible areas
cards.description: A person or human-like shape appears in a place that was previously empty.
cards.warningSigns: The figure remains motionless, watches the player, appears suddenly, or has unnatural features.
cards.recommendedAction: Do not approach it unnecessarily and follow the safest available route away from the area.

cards.title: Changed Furniture or Objects
cards.location: Living areas and rooms containing recognizable objects
cards.description: Furniture, decorations, or household items appear moved, replaced, missing, or visually altered.
cards.warningSigns: An object no longer matches its original position, shape, number, or appearance.
cards.recommendedAction: Treat the change as an active anomaly and avoid interacting with it unless progression requires it.

cards.title: Abnormal Face or Body
cards.location: Door encounters and close-range character appearances
cards.description: A character displays facial or physical details that do not look human or consistent.
cards.warningSigns: Distorted eyes, mouth, limbs, posture, proportions, or expressions.
cards.recommendedAction: Reject the visitor or move away from the character immediately.

cards.title: Lighting Change
cards.description: The brightness, color, or behavior of the lights changes without a normal cause.
cards.warningSigns: Sudden darkness, flickering, uneven lighting, or a room looking different from the previous inspection.
cards.recommendedAction: Inspect the area from a safe distance and check nearby rooms for additional changes.

cards.title: Window Anomaly
cards.location: Windows visible from inside the house
cards.description: A figure, movement, or visual detail appears outside or directly against a window.
cards.warningSigns: A face, silhouette, unexplained motion, or object appears where nothing was visible before.
cards.recommendedAction: Do not move directly toward the window and remain alert for a related encounter inside.

cards.title: Door Anomaly
cards.description: A door behaves differently, reveals something unusual, or becomes connected to a suspicious visitor event.
cards.warningSigns: Unexpected opening, repeated knocking, unusual sounds, or a dangerous figure visible nearby.
cards.recommendedAction: Keep the entrance secured and inspect before choosing any interaction.

cards.title: Unexplained Sound
cards.description: Footsteps, knocking, movement, or another sound occurs without a visible source.
cards.warningSigns: The sound repeats, moves between rooms, or begins immediately after a visitor encounter.
cards.recommendedAction: Check nearby areas carefully without rushing toward the source.

cards.title: Environment No Longer Matches
cards.description: Multiple details in a room change together, making the space feel noticeably different from its original state.
cards.warningSigns: Altered objects, lighting, figures, or room details appear during the same inspection.
cards.recommendedAction: Leave the area, avoid suspicious interactions, and continue only after identifying a safe route.

endingsGuide.title: Home Alone Anomaly Endings Guide
endingsGuide.subtitle: The final outcome depends on surviving the visitor encounters and responding correctly to dangerous anomalies.

items.heading: How do you reach the main ending in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Complete the night sequence while keeping the house secure, handling visitor encounters safely, and surviving each anomaly event. Continue following the inspection cycle until the final scripted scene begins.

items.heading: What happens if you trust the wrong visitor in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Allowing a suspicious or abnormal visitor to gain access can trigger a dangerous encounter and end the run before the final scene. Check appearance, dialogue, and behavior before making the decision.

items.heading: Can a familiar-looking person be dangerous in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Yes. Familiarity is not enough to confirm that a visitor is safe. Unnatural features, inconsistent dialogue, or strange behavior should take priority over the visitor's appearance.

items.heading: What causes an early failure in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Common causes include opening the door for an unsafe visitor, approaching a threatening figure, ignoring an obvious environmental change, or selecting a risky interaction during an encounter.

items.heading: Why should you inspect the house repeatedly in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: New anomalies can appear after visitor conversations and other scripted events. Rechecking the same rooms allows the player to notice changes before entering a dangerous area.

items.heading: What is the safest visitor strategy in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Keep the door closed until the visitor's appearance, dialogue, and reason for arriving all make sense. Refuse entry whenever one of those details appears abnormal or contradictory.

items.heading: How do you survive the final sequence in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Avoid unnecessary exploration, maintain distance from suspicious figures, and continue choosing the safest available responses. Do not assume the danger has ended until the final scene has fully started.

items.heading: What should you do after a failed run in Home Alone Anomaly?
items.content: Repeat the night while focusing on the encounter that caused the failure. Memorize the normal room layout, slow down during visitor dialogue, and inspect the house after every major event.

beginnerGuide.title: Home Alone Anomaly Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn the basic survival loop, inspect the house, and handle visitors without rushing into dangerous choices.

steps.title: Learn the House Layout
steps.description: Walk through the accessible rooms before focusing on visitors. Remember the route between the main living areas and the front door so you can investigate changes without getting lost.

steps.title: Inspect the Starting Environment
steps.description: Look around the house and note what appears normal at the beginning of the night. This gives you a reference point for recognizing objects, sounds, or room details that change later.

steps.title: Approach Visitors Carefully
steps.description: When someone arrives, do not immediately let them inside. Move to the door, observe the visitor, and wait for the available interaction or dialogue before deciding.

steps.title: Look for Anomalies
steps.description: Compare the visitor's appearance, behavior, and statements with what you have already seen. Strange details or inconsistent information can indicate that the encounter is dangerous.

steps.title: Make the Door Decision
steps.description: Choose whether to trust or reject the visitor only after completing your inspection. Allowing the wrong person inside or rejecting an important visitor can change the outcome of the night.

steps.title: Repeat the Survival Loop
steps.description: After each encounter, check the house again and prepare for the next visitor. Continue observing, comparing, and deciding until the current sequence reaches its ending.

survivalTips.title: Home Alone Anomaly Survival Tips
survivalTips.subtitle: Use these practical checks to identify suspicious visitors, notice changes inside the house, and avoid preventable deaths.

cards.title: Never Decide Immediately
cards.description: Wait until you have inspected the visitor and read the full conversation. A rushed door choice can end the run before important warning signs become clear.

cards.title: Check the Visitor's Appearance
cards.description: Look closely for unusual facial features, body details, movement, or other visual inconsistencies before allowing anyone into the house.

cards.title: Read Every Dialogue Line
cards.description: Visitor dialogue can provide clues about identity, intentions, or contradictions. Skipping text makes suspicious encounters harder to judge.

cards.title: Compare Repeated Encounters
cards.description: A familiar visitor should not automatically be trusted. Compare their current appearance and behavior with earlier encounters because small differences may matter.

cards.title: Recheck the House
cards.description: Environmental changes can appear between visitor encounters. Inspect nearby rooms and visible objects instead of remaining at the door for the entire night.

cards.title: Use Sound as a Warning
cards.description: Pay attention to knocks, movement, and other audio cues. Unexpected sounds can signal a new visitor or a change somewhere inside the house.

cards.title: Remember Previous Clues
cards.description: Keep track of what visitors said and how the house looked earlier. Later choices may depend on recognizing a contradiction or repeated anomaly.

cards.title: Stay Near a Clear Route
cards.description: Learn the shortest route between the main rooms and the entrance. Moving confidently through the house makes repeated inspections faster and safer.

controlsAndMechanics.title: Home Alone Anomaly Controls and Mechanics
controlsAndMechanics.subtitle: Understand movement, interaction, visitor inspection, dialogue decisions, and the core encounter loop.

rows.purpose: Walk through the house and approach interactive areas.
rows.mechanic: Movement is used to inspect rooms, return to the entrance, and investigate changes.

rows.input: Mouse movement or right analog stick
rows.purpose: Examine visitors, objects, and the surrounding environment.
rows.mechanic: Careful visual inspection helps reveal suspicious details and environmental anomalies.

rows.purpose: Use doors, examine available objects, and begin visitor interactions.
rows.mechanic: Interaction prompts appear when the player is close enough to a usable object or encounter point.

rows.input: Approach the entrance and use the available prompt
rows.purpose: Observe the person waiting outside before making a decision.
rows.mechanic: The door encounter is the main point where visitor information and visual clues are presented.

rows.input: Select or confirm the displayed dialogue option
rows.purpose: Continue conversations and reveal additional information.
rows.mechanic: Dialogue can contain clues, contradictions, and information needed to judge a visitor.

rows.input: Select one of the displayed choices
rows.purpose: Decide how to handle the current visitor or event.
rows.mechanic: Choices affect whether a visitor is accepted, rejected, or handled through another available response.

rows.input: Move between rooms and inspect visible details
rows.purpose: Look for changes that appeared after an encounter.
rows.mechanic: The house can provide additional warning signs beyond the visitor standing at the door.

rows.purpose: Advance to the next visitor or story event.
rows.mechanic: The survival loop repeats through inspection, dialogue, decision-making, and environmental checking.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Home Alone Anomaly

questions.question: What is Home Alone Anomaly?
questions.answer: Home Alone Anomaly is a Roblox doorstep horror survival game where you inspect visitors, spot anomalies, and decide who to trust in order to survive the night.

questions.question: How do I survive in Home Alone Anomaly?
questions.answer: Inspect every visitor carefully, read all dialogue, compare the house with its normal state, and keep the door closed whenever a detail feels wrong.

questions.question: Who should I trust in Home Alone Anomaly?
questions.answer: Treat every visitor as suspicious until their appearance, dialogue, and reason for arriving all stay consistent. Familiarity alone is never enough to confirm a visitor is safe.

questions.question: What are anomalies in Home Alone Anomaly?
questions.answer: Anomalies are changes inside or around the house, such as unexpected figures, moved objects, lighting shifts, or unexplained sounds, that warn a dangerous event may be near.

questions.question: How many endings does Home Alone Anomaly have?
questions.answer: Home Alone Anomaly builds toward a final scene that unlocks when you survive the visitor encounters and anomaly events without inviting danger inside.

questions.question: Where can I play Home Alone Anomaly?
questions.answer: Home Alone Anomaly is a free Roblox experience you can play through the official Roblox game page on any supported device.
